etymology.description: 笨(笨) (bèn, "stupid; clumsy") refers to lack of intelligence or dexterity. 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") and 腳(腳) (jiǎo, "foot") refer to the physical limbs for manipulation and movement. The combination of 笨(笨) (bèn, "clumsy") with 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") and 腳(腳) (jiǎo, "foot") conveys unskillfulness and lack of coordination in using one's hands and feet, leading to the overall meaning of being "clumsy" or "awkward."
etymology.interpretation: 笨(笨) (bèn, "clumsy") in conjunction with 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") directly paints the picture of someone who is awkward in handling or manipulating objects. Similarly, 笨(笨) (bèn, "clumsy") with 腳(腳) (jiǎo, "foot") creates the image of a person who is unsteady on their feet. Collectively, these depict someone whose awkwardness extends from precise manipulation to general movement, effectively embodying the essence of clumsiness or awkwardness.

examples.chinese: 他总是笨手笨脚地打破东西(他總是笨手笨腳地打破東西)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì bènshǒu bènjiǎo de dǎpò dōngxī.

examples.chinese: 她笨手笨脚，但非常可爱(她笨手笨腳，但非常可愛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bènshǒu bènjiǎo, dàn fēicháng kě'ài.
examples.english: She is clumsy but very adorable.

examples.chinese: 小明在体育课上显得笨手笨脚(小明在體育課上顯得笨手笨腳)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎomíng zài tǐyù kè shàng xiǎndé bènshǒu bènjiǎo.
examples.english: Xiaoming appears clumsy in gym class.
